title: plagiobothrys humistratus
intro: Plagiobothrys humistratus, also known as Sand Balsam or Desert Balsam, is a small, annual or perennial plant that belongs to the borage family and is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help soothe and heal wounds, as well as reduce fever and alleviate respiratory issues. The health benefits of Plagiobothrys humistratus include its ability to aid in the treatment of bronchitis, pneumonia, and other respiratory infections, as well as its use as a natural antiseptic and antibacterial agent. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and roots are typically dried and used in teas, infusions, and salves to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
